machinery_genes.quote: it is shown here that epsC, epsF, epsG, and epsL also encode proteins essential for GSP function
machinery_genes.note: Named here as a component of the V. cholerae general secretion pathway (Eps system); GspC-family inner-membrane platform role from the apparatus brief vocabulary.

machinery_genes.quote: Protein D is a member of the secretin family that includes proteins required for type IV pilus biogenesis, filamentous phage extrusion, and type III secretion... present in the outer membrane, where they are thought to form gated secretion pores
machinery_genes.note: Sandkvist 2001 review of the V. cholerae Eps system; 'protein D' = EpsD.

machinery_genes.quote: EpsE functions to couple energy to the type II apparatus, thus enabling secretion.
machinery_genes.note: Camberg & Sandkvist 2005 demonstrate purified EpsE is an Mg2+-dependent ATPase of the V. cholerae T2SS.

machinery_genes.quote: it is shown here that epsC, epsF, epsG, and epsL also encode proteins essential for GSP function
machinery_genes.note: Named as essential Eps (GSP) component; GspF-family IM platform role from apparatus brief vocabulary.

machinery_genes.quote: a pilin-like protein, EpsG, was analyzed also in V. cholerae and found to migrate as two bands
machinery_genes.note: Pilin-like (prepilin/pseudopilin) subunit of the Eps apparatus; Sandkvist 2001 groups proteins G, H, I, J, K as type IV prepilin-like subunits.

machinery_genes.quote: proteins G, H, I, J, and K are similar to the type IV prepilin subunits with respect to their size and sequence similarity within the N-terminal domains
machinery_genes.note: Sandkvist 2001, V. cholerae Eps system; 'protein H' = EpsH.

machinery_genes.quote: proteins G, H, I, J, and K are similar to the type IV prepilin subunits with respect to their size and sequence similarity within the N-terminal domains
machinery_genes.note: Sandkvist 2001; 'protein I' = EpsI. Independently confirmed as a substrate of the VcpD/PilD prepilin peptidase (see VcpD entry).

machinery_genes.quote: proteins G, H, I, J, and K are similar to the type IV prepilin subunits with respect to their size and sequence similarity within the N-terminal domains
proteins G, H, I, J, and K are similar to the type IV prepilin subunits with respect to their size and sequence similarity within the N-terminal domains
the cytoplasmic membrane proteins EpsL and EpsM of Vibrio cholerae
machinery_genes.note: Sandkvist et al 1999; EpsL has a large cytoplasmic domain and interacts with the autophosphorylating cytoplasmic membrane protein EpsE.

machinery_genes.quote: the cytoplasmic membrane proteins EpsL and EpsM of Vibrio cholerae
machinery_genes.note: Sandkvist et al 1999 show EpsL and EpsM form a direct, detergent-stable complex in the V. cholerae secretion apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: a DNA fragment from V. cholerae, containing 12 open reading frames, epsC to -N
machinery_genes.note: epsN is the terminal gene of the eps (epsC-N) operon named in the original cloning/sequencing paper as a GSP gene; no separate per-gene functional characterization quote was found, role assigned from GspN accessory family in the apparatus brief. Lowest-confidence machinery call in this set.

machinery_genes.role: prepilin peptidase (matures pseudopilins; shared with T4 pilus)
machinery_genes.quote: VcpD (PilD) is required for CT secretion via the Eps apparatus and has been shown to process the precursor form of EpsI as well as the prepilin forms
machinery_genes.note: VcpD = PilD; the V. cholerae prepilin peptidase. Encoded outside the epsC-N operon but tied to the Eps T2SS apparatus: it matures the Eps pseudopilins (EpsI). Synonymous with GspO.

notes: V. cholerae N16961 encodes a single Eps T2SS apparatus (the epsC-N operon on chromosome 1, VC genes), characterized by Sandkvist and colleagues. This instance (T2SS_02) shares the same sys_instance_label 'Eps (epsC-N)' and organism as T2SS_01; the difference is the curated effector set, which here lists chromosome-2 (VCA) substrates secreted by this same single Eps machine. The apparatus gene list is therefore identical to T2SS_01 and is supported by the same four Sandkvist-lab papers; there is no separate chromosome-2-encoded T2SS apparatus in this genome. Core apparatus fully covered: secretin (EpsD), ATPase (EpsE), IM platform (EpsC, EpsF, EpsL, EpsM), major + minor pseudopilins (EpsG, EpsH, EpsI, EpsJ, EpsK), prepilin peptidase (VcpD/PilD). EpsN included as the terminal operon gene (accessory GspN) but lacks an individual functional-characterization quote; flagged as lowest confidence. VcpD/PilD is encoded outside epsC-N (shared with the type IV pilus system) but documented as required for Eps-mediated secretion and processing of the Eps pseudopilins. Sec/Tat translocon, regulators, and secreted substrates (cholera toxin, the VCA effectors, etc.) deliberately excluded as non-machinery. All PMIDs/DOIs verified via PubMed get_article_metadata; all quotes verbatim from the cited PMC full texts or abstracts.
